title: Codariocalyx gyroides
intro: Codariocalyx gyroides, commonly known as the "Indian pipe cactus" or "gyroides," is a perennial, herbaceous plant belonging to the family Fabaceae. This plant has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine to treat various health issues, including fever, diarrhea, and inflammation, due to its potential health benefits in reducing oxidative stress and promoting wound healing. The bioactive constituents of Codariocalyx gyroides include alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Traditionally, the plant is used to make herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and decoctions, which are often used to treat digestive issues and fever.
taxonomy: Codariocalyx gyroides is a genus of flowering plants in the family Fabaceae. It belongs to the tribe Desmodieae. The classification of Codariocalyx gyroides includes its kingdom as Plantae and its class as Magnoliopsida. Within the class Magnoliopsida, it falls under the order Fabales and the family Fabaceae. Furthermore, it is categorized as a species within the genus Codariocalyx.
uses: Codariocalyx gyroides, also known as the climbing perch, has been used in traditional medicine for various purposes. In traditional Indian medicine, it is used to treat fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues. The plant is also believed to have antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which may help to soothe skin irritations and reduce swelling. Additionally, it is used to treat respiratory problems, such as cough and bronchitis. Further research is needed to fully understand the potential medicinal uses of Codariocalyx gyroides.

parts: Codariocalyx gyroides is also known as stick plant or climbing stick. The plant has been found to have various medicinal properties, with its leaves being used in traditional medicine. preparations: Codariocalyx gyroides, also known as stick-tiger or climbing cat grass, has been used in traditional herbal medicine in various parts of the world.
